specs.reason: Provides general background context and mapping of the directory structure to specs.
Provides context on Builtins Conformance (Python 3.12) which relates to the stdlib testing and xfail resolution goals.
Mentions past xfail cleanup procedures (e.g. match/case XFAIL cleanup), which provides context on how xfails are managed.
Background context on native bindings and configuration; not directly tied to the conformance xfails.
Related to crate wiring and schema binding; not relevant to the specific parser/codegen/resolution bugs in the xfails.
Describes new features like string escapes, slicing, and global/nonlocal, but the specific bugs (f-strings, walrus, codegen IR) are better covered by the component-specific specs.
Focused entirely on pattern matching; not one of the targeted conformance fixes.
Directly relevant to Q2 (Codegen IR root cause). Covers Cranelift backend, function signature generation, and CallExtern which relate to the calling-convention mismatches.
Directly relevant to Q3 (Stdlib non-iterable returns). Covers HIR to MIR lowering where module function calls and CallExtern are handled.
Directly relevant to Q4 (Parser nested f-string approach). Covers expression parsing and f-string interpolation.
Directly relevant to Q4. Covers the lexer-level changes needed for nested f-strings.
Directly relevant to Q5 (Walrus scope fix layer). Covers the symbol-table construction pass (resolve/pass.rs) where the walrus operator scope issue needs fixing.
